description: Evidence-grounded AI-assisted workflow for transforming current issues into Quran-centred short-form dakwah media under mandatory human review. Sources: approved QUL downloaded datasets (Quran, translation, tafsir, topics) and human-assisted SemakHadis.com verification. Evidence first, reflection second, publication only after human review.
ownerBoundary: assess current issues for reflective dakwah content
map human issues to Islamic themes
retrieve Quran and tafsir evidence from approved QUL datasets
record optional hadith verification from Semak Hadis
validate scripts against evidence and safety rules
guardrails: Arabic Quran text must come from the configured QUL dataset.
AI must not invent a Quran reference, translation, tafsir quotation, hadith, source, or news fact.
AI thematic matching is advisory and must be verified by a human.
No hadith may appear in a final script while verification is pending.
AI must not claim that a modern event is the direct reason for revelation of an ayah.
AI must not claim that a disaster or tragedy is divine punishment.
Religious review and editorial publication approval are mandatory and separate.
The pack must not publish automatically in v1.

verification.manifest: draft — Phase A skeleton per QMCP Blueprint V1.0, not yet reviewed by eff
verification.runtime: 13/13 executors implemented (Phase B deterministic evidence nodes + Phase C AI editorial nodes + Phase D current-issue research, wired to ReligiousSourceService/AiService/CurrentIssueResearchService in apps/api/src/execution/real-nodes/index.ts per QMCP Volume 2). discover_current_issues degrades to the honest RESEARCH_SERVICE_NOT_CONFIGURED stub whenever no approved RSS/news source is registered (CURRENT_ISSUE_RESEARCH_SOURCES) — the source allowlist is a content-governance decision for eff, same posture as the QUL dataset pick. Verified via sandbox tsc build against a real local HTTP fixture feed (not reimplemented logic). Not yet typechecked/tested on a live install (sandbox cross-mount symlink limitation — pending eff's Windows pnpm build:packs/typecheck/test pass).
verification.templates: draft — two importable templates. issue-to-dakwah-video: the main chain with all three human gates wired, plus a real Video Production handoff (prepare_media_brief -> read_script -> draft_scenes, via new scriptText/title ports) and a real human-facing Gate R on the revision branch (replacing a passive log). issue-to-dakwah-video-revision: companion template that re-runs only compose_reflection->write_short_video_script->validate_dakwah_content against an already Gate-2-approved bundle -- the execution engine is a strict DAG (verified via graph-planner.ts's Kahn's-algorithm cycle detection, CYCLE_DETECTED blocks the whole run), so a same-graph loop-back edge is not possible; this is the real substitute, re-triggerable by an operator. Both graphs verified structurally (zero cycles, zero unknown node types, zero port-id mismatches, every node reachable) via the real compiled graph-planner.ts against the real cross-pack port registry (workflow-foundation + human-work + video-production + quran-media). Optional hadith verification remains a standalone example node (see templates/README.md). Not yet sanity-checked with a real live import + trigger on eff's Windows install.
